local_compute_review.summary: 4 attack categories completed. All results consistent with existing framework. Two notable signals identified — one tautological (lag-38 from Bean), one pre-existing (lag-7). No new cipher-family signal.

assessment.lag7: Non-significant after Bonferroni correction (E-FRAC-14). Already known.
assessment.lag38: TAUTOLOGICAL ARTIFACT: CT[27]=CT[65]='P' creates lag-38 autocorrelation by definition. Additional lag-38 matches: (15,53)=L/L, (20,58)=W/W, (36,74)=W/W, (57,95)=A/A — 5 total vs. expected 2.3. Slightly elevated but explained by Bean constraint. NOT a period-38 signal.
assessment.lag50: CT[2]=K=CT[52] and CT[12]=B=CT[62]. Only 2 matches out of 47 pairs at lag 50 (expected 1.8). Statistical noise.
assessment.lag43_41: No obvious CT-repeat structure at these lags. Noise.
assessment.conclusion: No actionable signal from local compute statistical profile. All autocorrelation peaks are noise or tautological.

simple_ciphers.assessment: Caesar + Affine eliminated. Redundant with framework Tier-1 eliminations. No new information.

keywords.assessment: Consistent with framework. Keyword Vigenere/Quagmire comprehensively eliminated.

columnar.note: Framework covered widths 5-15 exhaustively. The '0 candidates' in local compute likely means Bean filter eliminated all configs before crib scoring — consistent with E-FRAC-35. Widths 2-4 trivially add nothing. No new information.

new_analytical_finding_1.title: Complete period elimination sweep: ALL periods 2-52 eliminated; periods 27, 28, 29 close the last gap
new_analytical_finding_1.classification: [DERIVED FACT — partially new, partially extending E-FRAC-35/E-AUDIT-01]
new_analytical_finding_1.method: For each period p from 2 to 96, check whether any two crib positions share residue mod p but have different required key values.

results.periods_2_26: Eliminated by E-FRAC-35 (Bean proof) and E-AUDIT-01 (pairwise constraints). Confirmed here.
results.periods_30_52: Eliminated by direct crib-consistency conflict. E.g., p=30: pos33 and pos63 both at residue 3 mod 30, but key[33]=25 ≠ key[63]=12. Every period in 30-52 has at least one such conflict.

periods_27_28_29.status: Survive crib-consistency AND Bean (new finding — not covered by E-FRAC-35 which only covers p=2-26)
periods_27_28_29.but: Applying the crib-derived key to K4 yields 84-86 known plaintext positions that spell pure gibberish (quadgram ~ -6.5/char = random baseline). Eliminated by readability.
periods_27_28_29.example_p27: PT under period-27 Vigenere: QDEPKOY??VANRHIBUOB?EASTNORTHEAST??KZSIYKIENXZ?VPUHHJ WFCYMIO??BERLINCLOCK?FSLXURLEXGWKV??OAGARUH. Non-crib portions are random noise.
periods_27_28_29.formal_test: scripts/e_period_27_29_formal.py — runs all 3 cipher variants × 26^3 free residues, checks quadgram signal

results.periods_53_96: Underdetermined: no two crib positions share a residue, so the key is unconstrained at all but 24 positions. Subsumed by the running-key underdetermination result (E-FRAC-39/44).

new_analytical_finding_1.implication: ALL periodic keys with periods 2-52 are now eliminated under direct positional correspondence (no transposition). Combined with E-FRAC-35 covering ALL 97! transpositions + periodic key at p=2-26, the only remaining periodic hypotheses require transposition AND period > 26 — but all such models are underdetermined (E-FRAC-44, ~2^138 parameter freedom).

new_analytical_finding_2.title: Lag-38 autocorrelation peak is a tautological artifact of the Bean equality constraint
new_analytical_finding_2.reasoning: CT[27]=CT[65]='P' by the Bean constraint (both are ciphertext 'P'). This creates a guaranteed autocorrelation at lag 38 (position 27 and 65 are separated by 38). The local compute report lists lag-38 as a 'possible key length' — this is incorrect. It is not evidence of period-38 structure; it is a mathematical consequence of CT[27]=CT[65].
new_analytical_finding_2.recommendation: Remove lag-38 from all future statistical reports as a structural artifact, not a signal.

retained_from_prior_analysis.title: Keystream letter-frequency anomalies (prior agent finding, validated)

anomalies.vigenere: Z×2 (119× English rate), K×3 (16×), Y×3 (6×). Vowel ratio: 4/24=16.7% (English: ~40%).
anomalies.beaufort: K×5 across 24 positions (expected ~0.18 under English). K~7.2% in Finnish → K×5 ≈ 2.3% (920× more likely than English).
anomalies.var_beaufort: Q×3 (125× English rate), Y×3 (6×). Vowel ratio: 5/24=20.8%.

retained_from_prior_analysis.significance: ALL three cipher variants show elevated Q/K/Y/Z. These letters are prominently used in Egyptological transliteration (Q=qoph, K=kaph, Y=yod, Z=zayin). The anomaly is TRANSPOSITION-INDEPENDENT — no transposition can explain the anomaly if the key source text is English. P(coincidence under English) < 10^-7.
retained_from_prior_analysis.implication: Running key source is almost certainly NOT English/German/French. Candidates: Slavic languages (Polish, Czech, Z/K-heavy), Finnish (K-heavy), or Egyptological transliteration (Q/K/Y/Z-heavy from hieroglyph romanization).
retained_from_prior_analysis.caveat: This finding depends on the running key model being correct. If the cipher is bespoke/procedural, the key values are not character frequencies from a natural-language source.

next_steps.title: FORMAL: Close the period-27/28/29 gap (last surviving periodic-key candidates)
next_steps.rationale: Periods 27, 28, 29 are the ONLY periods 2-52 not covered by E-FRAC-35 (p=2-26) or crib-consistency elimination (p=30-52). They survive abstract constraint tests but are eliminated by readability of the determined plaintext positions. This script formally documents the result.
next_steps.expected_outcome: All three periods ELIMINATED (quadgram ~ -6.5/char). New [DERIVED FACT]: ALL periodic keys p=2..52 eliminated under identity transposition.
next_steps.novelty: Fills an explicit gap in E-FRAC-35's scope (p=2-26 only). No existing script in the 320+ covers p=27-29.

next_steps.title: NOVEL: Weltzeituhr city-name sequence and UTC offsets as running key source
rationale: Sanborn's Aug 2025 clue: BERLINCLOCK = Weltzeituhr. Called it 'a reminder.'
The Weltzeituhr (Alexanderplatz, 1969) has ~148 cities in 24 time-zone sectors — publicly visible, non-mathematical, position-dependent.
Prior tests used Weltzeituhr only as TRANSPOSITION source (Operation Final Vector: 377 configs). Never tested as KEY SOURCE.
City names concatenated (German GDR-era spelling) = ~900 alpha chars → test all ~800 offsets.
UTC offsets (offset+12 mod 26) = ~148 numeric key values → test with EAST filter.
Framework tested 73.7M chars of general text (E-CFM-09) but NOT this specific sequence.
next_steps.expected_outcome: Likely NOISE (city names are too specific to accidentally match BLZCDCYYGCKAZ). But critical to test given Sanborn's explicit clue.
next_steps.novelty: First test of Weltzeituhr city sequence as KEY SOURCE (not transposition). Directly motivated by Sanborn's 2025 disclosure.

next_steps.title: NOVEL: All order-2 linear recurrence key models — exhaustive sweep
rationale: E-FRAC-38 eliminated three specific recurrences: Fibonacci (a=b=1), progressive (b=0), quadratic polynomial.
E-FRAC-38's claim 'running key is the ONLY structured model surviving Bean' is based on only these three cases.
General homogeneous recurrence key[n] = (a×key[n-1] + b×key[n-2]) mod 26 covers 676 (a,b) pairs × 676 seeds = 456,976 configs.
Many (a,b) rules are hand-computable (multiply + add — fits 1989 pre-computer context).
If NOISE: extends E-FRAC-38's elimination from 3 specific cases to ALL order-2 linear recurrences.
If SIGNAL: potential breakthrough identifying a compact key generation rule.
next_steps.expected_outcome: NOISE — Bean + crib constraints will filter out essentially all seeds. If any 24/24 match: immediate follow-up.
next_steps.novelty: Fills an explicit gap left by E-FRAC-38. Tests 456,976 configurations per variant not previously enumerated.

additional_hypotheses_for_future.title: High-Z/K language corpus scan (Polish, Czech, Finnish, Hungarian)
additional_hypotheses_for_future.rationale: Beaufort key K×5 (P<10^-5 under English) is 920× more consistent with Finnish. Polish and Czech have Z rates 80-120× English. Fits the transposition-independent frequency anomaly.
additional_hypotheses_for_future.action: Extend E-CFM-09-style scan to Slavic language Gutenberg books + Finnish texts. Use EAST constraint filter.

additional_hypotheses_for_future.title: Egyptological transliteration running key
additional_hypotheses_for_future.rationale: All three cipher variants show elevated Q/K/Y/Z consistent with hieroglyph romanization. Sanborn's 1986 Egypt trip. Carter's Tutankhamun connection. Budge's Book of the Dead has extensive Q/K/Y-heavy transliteration text.
texts: Budge: Book of the Dead (Project Gutenberg #5359)
Budge Egyptian Hieroglyphic Dictionary word list
additional_hypotheses_for_future.action: Add to running-key corpus scan with EAST filter
additional_hypotheses_for_future.effort: Low (texts are short, ~50K chars total; can run in minutes)

additional_hypotheses_for_future.title: Non-identity transposition + Gutenberg (47.4M chars): EAST-filtered sweep
additional_hypotheses_for_future.rationale: E-CFM-09 tested 47.4M chars under identity transposition only. The framework also tested columnar widths 6/8/9 with 7 known texts (E-FRAC-49/50). Not yet done: full 47.4M Gutenberg corpus + columnar width 7 (the only meaningful autocorrelation period ≤ 7).
additional_hypotheses_for_future.action: Run e_egypt_01-style columnar sweep at width 7 against the 47.4M-char English corpus
additional_hypotheses_for_future.effort: High (days of compute for full exhaustive test)

scripts_written_this_session.description: Tests periods 27, 28, 29 — the only periodic key candidates not covered by E-FRAC-35 or prior crib-consistency analysis. Derives plaintext for known residue positions, checks quadgram score, searches free residue values.

Tests Weltzeituhr city names (German spelling, UTC-offset order) and UTC offset sequences as running key sources. Includes EAST constraint pre-filter, all 3 cipher variants, clockwise/counterclockwise orderings, single time-zone rings.

Exhaustively tests all 676 (a,b) pairs × 676 seeds for homogeneous order-2 linear recurrences key[n]=(a×key[n-1]+b×key[n-2]) mod 26. Fills the gap left by E-FRAC-38's three specific cases. Uses multiprocessing.

key_constraints_reference.scoring_note: Only scores at period ≤7 are meaningful discriminators. All high-period scores are false positives.
